articles.title: Israel to extend detention of Gaza flotilla activists
articles.summary: Israel to extend detention of Gaza flotilla activists Israel will request a six-day extension of the detention of the Gaza flotilla activists who were arrested off the coast of Greece. The hearing is scheduled for Tuesday in Ashkelon where Spanish activist Saif Abu Keshek and Brazilian activist Thiago Avila are being held. "Our lawyers have been informed that the state is requesting a six-day extension," said Miriam Azem, head of the Adalah rights group, speaking to AFP. The Israeli court approved a first two-day extension of their detention on Sunday.

articles.title: Morning update
articles.summary: Morning update Good morning Middle East Eye readers, The situation across the Middle East is becoming more volatile, with rising tensions in the Strait of Hormuz and renewed pressure along the Israel-Lebanon border. Donald Trump’s maritime mission is moving ahead with added military support, while Tehran is warning of a “crushing” response to any US actions it sees as a breach. At the same time, Israel has raised its alert level and is preparing for a possible expansion of operations, adding to concerns that the fragile ceasefire could give way to wider conflict. articles.title: Israeli military orders evacuations in two Lebanon towns
articles.summary: Israeli military orders evacuations in two Lebanon towns The Israeli military ordered residents of Jabsheet and Sarafand in southern Lebanon to leave their homes, issuing fresh displacement warnings amid ongoing tensions. Residents were told to evacuate “immediately and move away from the villages and towns for a distance of at least 1,000 metres to open areas”. The military added that anyone remaining near what it described as “Hezbollah elements, facilities and combat” would be putting their lives at risk.

articles.title: Report: 15,000 Pakistanis arrested and deported from UAE, savings seized
articles.summary: Report: 15,000 Pakistanis arrested and deported from UAE, savings seized New Lines Magazine reported that authorities in the United Arab Emirates have detained and deported up to 15,000 Pakistani workers, many of them Shia Muslims, without formal charges. The report said the actions followed a pattern of arrests, phone confiscations, transfers between detention sites and deportation flights to Pakistan. The workers were sent back "without being given the opportunity to withdraw their funds" from banks, the report cited a Shia cleric as saying.  Many of those expelled had spent decades wor

articles.title: Israel raises alert level, officials say awaiting US ‘green light’ to resume war
articles.summary: Israel raises alert level, officials say awaiting US ‘green light’ to resume war Israeli Broadcasting Corporation reported that Israel has raised its alert level following regional escalation after reported missile attacks on the United Arab Emirates. The report said security meetings were held to assess the situation, while additional US aircraft, including refuelling planes, have arrived in Israel. According to Channel 14, an Israeli official said: “We are ready to return to fighting immediately, and we are waiting for the green light from the United States.”

articles.title: Tehran signals ‘unbearable cost’ for US actions, calls security ‘non-negotiable’
articles.summary: Tehran signals ‘unbearable cost’ for US actions, calls security ‘non-negotiable’ Iranian defence council member Ali Akbar Ahmadian said Iran’s security is “non-negotiable” and accused the United States of disrupting global maritime and energy stability. “Our security is non-negotiable, and the Americans have disrupted global maritime and energy security," he said.  “Americans should know that our operations will change the equation and make the cost unbearable for them,” Ahmadian added as tensions continue over the Strait of Hormuz and regional security.

articles.summary: Flotilla crew says it expects interception as communications drop near Greek waters A group aboard a Gaza-bound aid flotilla released a live transmission while sailing around 95 nautical miles from Greece, saying they believe an interception may be imminent after losing contact with one of the vessels in the convoy. The flotilla says it is attempting to deliver humanitarian aid to Gaza and draw international attention to the ongoing blockade and the humanitarian situation there due to Israel's genocide. articles.title: UAE becomes first regional buyer of Brazilian military transport aircraft
articles.summary: UAE becomes first regional buyer of Brazilian military transport aircraft Brazilian aircraft manufacturer Embraer said it expects to secure additional defence deals in the Middle East after a landmark agreement with the United Arab Emirates for up to 20 C-390 military transport aircraft. According to Reuters, the deal, valued at about $1bn, includes 10 firm orders and 10 options, making the UAE the first regional buyer of the aircraft. “The Middle East is a strategic region for us,” said Bosco da Costa Junior, head of defence and security at Embraer. The UAE already operates transport aircraft
